rss.title: Scientists just solved a major mystery about how your brain stores memories
rss.snippet: Scientists have found that your brain separates memories into “what” and “where/when” using two different groups of neurons. One set responds to specific objects or people, while another tracks the context or situation. When you remember something correctly, these groups briefly connect and reconstr

rss.title: Scientists link childhood stress to lifelong digestive issues
rss.snippet: Early life stress may set the stage for long-term digestive problems by disrupting the gut-brain connection. Studies in both mice and thousands of children found links to symptoms like pain, constipation, and IBS. Scientists discovered that different biological pathways control different gut issues,

rss.title: Cannabis study finds THC can create false memories
rss.snippet: THC doesn’t just blur memories—it can create new ones that never happened. In a controlled experiment, cannabis users were much more likely to recall words that were never shown and struggled with tasks like remembering to do something later.
